text: This is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语健康报道节目。

text: Malaria control efforts currently depend on things like chemically treated bed nets and spraying against mosquitoes,
zh: 目前疟疾防控工作主要依靠经过化学处理的蚊帐和喷洒杀虫剂来对付蚊子，

text: but scientists keep trying to find other ways to prevent the disease.
zh: 但科学家们仍在不断寻找预防疟疾的其他方法。

text: A number of vaccines remain under development.
zh: 目前有多种疫苗正在研发中。

text: Most contain genetically engineered versions of a few proteins from the Plasmodium parasite.
zh: 大多数疫苗含有经过基因工程改造的疟原虫蛋白质片段。

text: Plasmodium is the organism that causes malaria.
zh: 疟原虫正是引发疟疾的病原体。

text: These modified proteins are designed to get the body's defenses to launch an immune response against the Plasmodium.
zh: 这些经过改造的蛋白质旨在促使人体防御系统对疟原虫发起免疫反应。

text: But the parasite contains thousands of proteins.
zh: 但这种寄生虫体内含有成千上万种蛋白质。

text: Another experimental vaccine includes a deactivated version of the entire parasite.
zh: 另一种实验性疫苗则采用了整个寄生虫的灭活版本。

text: Robert Seder is a researcher at the National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases near Washington.
zh: 罗伯特·塞德是华盛顿附近美国国家过敏症和传染病研究所的一名研究人员。

text: So instead of picking out one or two or three genes,
zh: 与其只挑选一两个或三个基因，

text: you have the potential for what we call breath, generating an immune response that would be broad rather than narrower.
zh: 你就有可能获得我们所说的‘广度’，从而产生一种更广泛而非狭窄的免疫反应。

text: Radiation is used to weaken the parasite so it cannot make people sick or get spread by a mosquito.
zh: 用辐射来削弱寄生虫，使其既不能致病，也无法通过蚊子传播。

text: To make the vaccine, scientists use the parasite at a time in its growth when the organism is called a sporozoite.
zh: 为了制造这种疫苗，科学家们利用寄生虫处于‘子孢子’阶段时的形态。

text: The idea has been known since the 1960s.
zh: 这一想法早在20世纪60年代就已为人所知。

text: But Mr. Sater says a discovery by a researcher at a vaccine company cleared the way for progress.
zh: 但塞德先生表示，一家疫苗公司的研究人员的发现为这项研究扫清了障碍。

text: The major breakthrough here was that my collaborator, Stephen Hoffman at Cenaria,
zh: 这里的重大突破在于，我的合作者——Sanaria公司的斯蒂芬·霍夫曼——

text: developed a method where he could isolate the sporozoites and purify them so that they could administer it as a vaccine to humans.
zh: 开发出了一种方法，可以分离并纯化子孢子，从而将其作为疫苗施用于人体。

text: And no one thought that that was possible.
zh: 此前没有人认为这是可能的。

text: But no one knew either if the weakened sporozoites would activate the immune system to protect against malaria.
zh: 但也没有人知道，这些被削弱的子孢子是否能够激活免疫系统以抵御疟疾。

text: So researchers tested it on volunteers and found that it was safe, but it was not very effective.
zh: 于是研究人员在志愿者身上进行了测试，发现该疫苗是安全的，但效果并不理想。

text: Only two out of 44 volunteers were protected when bitten by malaria -infected mosquitoes.
zh: 在44名志愿者中，只有两人在被感染疟疾的蚊子叮咬后获得了保护。

text: To find out why, the researchers tested the vaccine on laboratory animals.
zh: 为了找出原因，研究人员在实验动物身上测试了这种疫苗。

text: They decided that the problem was the way the vaccine had been given to the volunteers.
zh: 他们认为问题出在疫苗给志愿者接种的方式上。

text: It was injected into the skin to simulate the bite of a mosquito.
zh: 疫苗是注射到皮肤中的，以模拟蚊子叮咬的方式。

text: keto.
zh: keto。

text: Mr. Sater says it would have been more effective if it had been given directly into the blood.
zh: 塞德先生表示，如果直接注射到血液中，效果会更好。

text: However, vaccines are generally given by mouth or injected into the skin or muscle.
zh: 然而，疫苗通常是通过口服，或注射到皮肤或肌肉中给药的。

text: Having to inject it into the blood could make vaccination programs more difficult if the vaccine is approved for general use.
zh: 如果这种疫苗被批准广泛使用，必须注射到血液中这一点可能会使接种项目更加复杂。

text: For now, more testing is needed.
zh: 目前，还需要进行更多测试。

text: A report on the testing appeared in the journal Science.
zh: 一份关于该测试的报告发表在《科学》杂志上。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Carolyn Presutti.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语，我是卡罗琳·普雷苏蒂。
